title: Mosaic
description: An interactive computational-geometry studio: Voronoi by both half-plane intersection and Fortune's sweep-line, power (Laguerre) diagrams with their regular-triangulation dual, the farthest-point Voronoi diagram, Delaunay and constrained Delaunay (Lawson edge-flips), convex hulls by both monotone chain and Quickhull, the β-skeleton family (Gabriel↔RNG) and k-nearest graph alongside the full proximity-graph nesting, alpha shapes with an animated α sweep, Ruppert quality meshing, rotating-calipers metrics, Welzl's enclosing circle, largest empty circle, Lloyd and power-Lloyd relaxation, and a Spatial Search explorer — k-d trees and quadtrees driving live nearest-neighbour, k-nearest, orthogonal-range and point-location queries — the latter by both the Θ(√n) jump-and-walk and Seidel's O(log n) trapezoidal map with an animated search-DAG descent (each verified against brute force) — plus (1+ε)-approximate best-bin-first NN and a fractional-cascading 2-D range tree for O(log n + k) range reporting. A Curves studio animates the Morton (Z-order) and Hilbert space-filling curves and measures their locality; the Studio adds Yao/Θ/greedy/WSPD geometric spanners — the WSPD-spanner drawn from a well-separated pair decomposition over a fair-split tree — each with a measured dilation. Every algorithm is built from scratch, with a 178-check test suite.
